data.title: Eric Trump Claims Banks Hinder Cryptocurrency Legislation
data.description: Eric Trump, in a recent interview with Fox Business, expressed concerns that banks are actively obstructing legislative progress on cryptocurrency. According to NS3.AI, he alleges that traditional financial institutions are influencing the delay of crypto-friendly laws. This claim underscores the ongoing tensions between cryptocurrency advocates and the established banking sector.

data.title: Russia's Constitutional Court Recognizes Cryptocurrencies Under Property Laws
data.description: Russia's Constitutional Court has determined that cryptocurrencies fall under property laws, offering legal safeguards for crypto holders. According to NS3.AI, this decision emerged from a civil lawsuit concerning a loan of USDT stablecoins, where lower courts had previously rejected claims due to ambiguous legislation. The ruling establishes ownership rights, reduces the requirement to report crypto to tax authorities, and sets a legal precedent in anticipation of forthcoming regulatory adjustments in Russia.

data.title: Webinar on Responsible Capital Growth in Cryptocurrency Scheduled
data.description: An educational webinar focusing on responsible capital growth in cryptocurrency is being organized. According to NS3.AI, the event will emphasize risk awareness and practical strategies for investors. The session will explore market trends anticipated for 2025-2026, alongside techniques for risk assessment and diversification for those with limited capital. Attendees will also learn how to evaluate crypto projects for transparency and legitimacy. The event will conclude with a live Q&A session, providing participants an opportunity to engage directly with experts.

data.title: Bitcoin Transfer from Cumberland DRW to Anonymous Address
data.description: A significant Bitcoin transaction was recorded early today. According to ChainCatcher, data from Arkham indicates that at 01:26, 107.82 BTC were transferred from Cumberland DRW to an anonymous address starting with bc1qcy. The transaction highlights ongoing movements within the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: White House Signals Path for Banks to Go All-In on Crypto
data.description:
The U. S. government’s approach to crypto regulation may be nearing a turning point, with potential implications far beyond price action. David Sacks said banks are expected to get fully into crypto once market structure legislation is in place, signaling a possible shift in how tradfi interacts with digital assets
source: https://www.techgaged.com/white-house-signals-path-for-banks-to-go-all-in-on-crypto/

data.title: Winklevoss Twins Donate $1.2M to Zcash’s Shielded Labs Amid ECC Exodus
data.description:
Tyler and Cameron Winklevoss donated 3,221 ZEC (approximately $1. 2 million) to Shielded Labs on Tuesday, the Swiss-based nonprofit confirmed, marking their second contribution to the independent Zcash development organization. Zcash (ZEC) is currently trading around $368. 85 and is up about 3
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/winklevoss-twins-donate-1-2m-zcash-shielded-labs-amid-ecc-exodus/

data.title: Bitcoin independence debated as Coinbase CEO challenges central banker at Davos
data.description:

Attendees were waiting for US President Donald Trump to speak at the event, and many were anticipating his usual spontaneous remarks about foreign relations and trade policies when Brian Armstrong […]

data.title: Denmark Rejects U.S. Proposal to Purchase Greenland
data.description: Denmark's Foreign Minister Lars Løkke Rasmussen has officially turned down U.S. President Donald Trump's proposal to negotiate the acquisition of Greenland. According to NS3.AI, Trump had shown interest in purchasing Greenland during a speech at the World Economic Forum in Davos, highlighting the United States' ability to safeguard and develop the region. Rasmussen's refusal highlights the diplomatic delicacy of the offer and Greenland's strategic importance.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Futures Markets Face Major Liquidation
data.description: Cryptocurrency futures markets have seen substantial liquidation, with $108 million worth of futures contracts liquidated in the last hour. According to NS3.AI, total futures liquidations over the past 24 hours amounted to $838 million, highlighting significant market volatility and potential distress among traders. This trend points to increased uncertainty and pressure on leveraged positions across major exchanges.

data.title: Monero's Price Decline Sparks Concerns Amid Potential Recovery Signals
data.description: Monero's price recently experienced a significant 20% drop, briefly falling below $500, which led to panic among short-term traders. According to NS3.AI, despite the sharp decline, on-chain data reveals subdued selling pressure, positive funding rates, and sustained long interest, indicating a potential recovery for the cryptocurrency. Analysts emphasize Monero's strong long-term bullish pattern, noting critical support levels between $400 and $500. They caution that maintaining a position above this zone could signal a continuation of the bullish trend, while failure to hold could result in a deeper correction.

data.title: Hassett: The Fed's criticism of Trump is inconsistent with its independence.
data.description: White House National Economic Council Director Hassett stated that U.S. GDP growth is likely to exceed 5% for two consecutive quarters, and further deficit reduction is expected next year. He noted that Federal Reserve members seem eager to comment on everything, and that the Fed's criticism of Trump is inconsistent with its independence. He expressed satisfaction with the large number of excellent Fed candidates and anticipates the Fed's investigation will proceed rapidly. (Jinshi)

data.description: On Jan 21, 2026, 16:57 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 88,000 USDT and is now trading at 87,838.6875 USDT, with a narrowed 2.62% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: U.S. Supreme Court Likely to Deny Trump's Request to Remove Fed Governor Cook
data.description: The U.S. Supreme Court appears poised to reject U.S. President Donald Trump's request to immediately remove Federal Reserve Governor Cook. According to Odaily, market sources indicate that the court is unlikely to grant the request.

data.title: Solana's Network Activity Surges Amid AI Token Interest
data.description: Solana's blockchain has experienced a notable increase in network activity, driven by growing interest in AI-linked tokens. According to NS3.AI, the number of active addresses on the Solana network rose from 14.7 million to 18.9 million over the course of a week. This increase has surpassed general trends in the cryptocurrency market, fueled by heightened investor speculation surrounding AI-related projects.

data.title: Iran's Central Bank Utilizes Tether to Bolster Rial and Facilitate Trade
data.description: Iran's central bank acquired approximately $507 million in Tether (USDT) last year to strengthen its national currency, the rial, and manage international trade payments. According to NS3.AI, the acquisition was executed through two significant transactions in April and May 2023. The majority of the funds were transferred to the local exchange Nobitex. Following these transactions, conversions via a cross-chain bridge resulted in the outflow of USDT from the bank's wallet by the year's end.
